scope: FQ-07: visual asset pipeline with color fallback — art improves one asset at a time without ever breaking the current simple-color rendering.
summary: Data surface: data/visual_assets.json (asset_root art/generated; categories blocks/items/enemies/ui; per-id explicit path overrides; documented per-category target sizes). Convention: art/generated/<category>/<id>.png is picked up automatically when present. Loading: BlockRegistry.visual_texture uses Image.load_from_file + ImageTexture — deliberately not the Godot import system, so the plain non-editor runs this repo always uses see new art without an import pass (exported-PCK caveat documented; this repo never exports). Misses cache as null and every render site keeps its generated look: world._make_block_texture (image nearest-neighbor resized to the tile; tileset collision/occlusion derive from block data and are untouched), simple_threat._draw (centered sprite; damage as a subtractive red tint since overbright modulate clamps invisible in the compatibility renderer), and a new 5-slot hotbar item icon strip whose icons hide without art (the text hotbar is the fallback). Workflow: art/source_templates/ASSET_TEMPLATE.md carries naming rules (exact data ids), sizes with the resize-is-blocks-only clarification, prompt skeletons for local Ollama/image-model iteration (strictly outside the game and validation), and a review checklist; art/generated/* committed with .gitkeep; smoke temp art (smoke_tmp_*.png) gitignored. Validator: broken explicit references FAIL; convention gaps print INFO lines and never fail.
acceptance: Missing images do not crash and use the fallback: null lookups verified, generated block texture renders, hotbar icons hidden; the entire 169-check legacy suite runs on fallbacks since the repo ships zero art

At least one block and one item render from image when present: runtime-written magenta block PNG wins pixel-for-pixel and the generated color returns on removal (fq07_block_renders_from_image); item art lights its hotbar icon and hides on removal (fq07_item_renders_from_image); enemies supported as a bonus
Validator reports missing optional assets as informational: 17 INFO lines, exit 0; broken explicit visual_assets.json references still fail as data bugs
Asset naming/template docs are clear enough for manual one-by-one art passes (naming, sizes, prompts, checklist); Ollama required nowhere in the game or validation path
Smoke 173/173 (was 169) on windowed Godot 4.6.1; validator PASS; capsule doctor healthy; git diff --check clean; no temp art left in the tree
validation: python scripts/validate_repo.py -> PASS incl. visual assets schema + INFO-only optional-asset reporting
COHERONIA_SMOKE=1 waited windowed run -> 173/173 PASS incl. four fq07_* checks with runtime-written, self-cleaning test art
decisions: Image loading bypasses the Godot import system (Image.load_from_file) so plain non-editor runs pick up art immediately — consistent with how this repo is always run; the exported-build limitation is documented, not engineered around
Only blocks auto-resize mismatched art (tileset integrity); enemies draw at raw size and the template warns artists to match sizes exactly
Enemy damage on the art path uses a subtractive red tint (review fix): overbright modulate clamps to white in the compatibility renderer, which would have made damage invisible
Smoke test art uses gitignored smoke_tmp_* filenames routed through explicit visual_assets overrides — no collision with future real asset names, and the override path gets exercised (review fix)
Validator severity split: explicit reference broken = FAIL (data bug); convention path missing = INFO (art is incremental by design)

Recon agent skipped this round (token saving): every target surface was already read or quoted verbatim earlier in the session; the sonnet review agent still ran as the correction loop (no must-fix; 3 should-fix + 3 nits applied)
risks: The pipeline is smoke-proven but no real art exists yet; the first actual art pass (FQ-08/FQ-09 era) exercises it at scale
Block art binds at tileset build time (world entry); no hot-reload
Player and Town Hall visuals remain drawn shapes; image support for them is future work
next_action: FQ-08: block and enemy damage visuals.
